questions.question: Determine whether the function f: ℝ → ℝ defined by f(x) = x³ - x is injective, surjective, both, or neither. Justify your answer.
expectedPoints: Check f(0) = f(1) = f(-1) = 0, so not injective
For any y ∈ ℝ, equation x³ - x = y has a real solution, so surjective

questions.question: Let f: A → B and g: B → C be functions. Prove that if both f and g are injective, then g∘f is injective. Also provide an example where g∘f is injective but f is not.
expectedPoints: Since g is injective, f(a₁) = f(a₂)
Since f is injective, a₁ = a₂
Define f(1) = x, f(2) = x (not injective)
Define g(x) = p, g(y) = q, g(z) = q
Then (g∘f)(1) = p, (g∘f)(2) = p, so g∘f is not injective either
Alternative: f: ℝ → ℝ, f(x) = x² (not injective)
(g∘f)(x) = e^(x²) is not injective since even function
